task_completion.description: Navigate the GCompris menu to locate the 'Scales Board' activity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris category menu (Math -> Measures) is visible, showing the icon for the 'Scales Board' activity.
The 'Scales Board' activity is open and active on the screen, clearly displaying a balance scale and draggable weights.

task_completion.description: Successfully complete the first level of the activity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The level progress indicator (typically stars or a bar at the bottom of the screen) advances from level 1 to level 2, or a success animation/feedback is displayed for the first level.

task_completion.description: Successfully complete the second level of the activity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The level progress indicator advances to level 3, showing that two levels have been completed.

task_completion.description: Successfully complete the third level of the activity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The level progress indicator advances to level 4, confirming that at least 3 levels have been successfully completed.

integrity.description: The agent interacted exclusively with the GCompris graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GCompris application being used normally. There are no terminal windows or text editors open to modify save files or bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent actively played the game by interacting with the weights.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots capture weights being dragged and dropped onto the balance scale's mass areas, rather than levels being skipped instantly without interaction.

integrity.description: The agent did not use external scripts or terminal commands to solve or bypass the levels.
integrity.visual_evidence: Only the GCompris window and standard OS UI elements are visible. No terminal windows running solver scripts or automated bypass commands are present.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 'Scales Board' activity (internal name: scalesboard) is located in the 'Math' -> 'Measures' section of GCompris (not Science/Experiment). It features a balance scale and draggable weights. The activity has 8 levels in total. Completion of a level is typically indicated by the level progress bar/stars advancing at the bottom of the screen. To verify completion of 3 levels, the progress indicator must reach at least level 4.
